channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Lawmakers push to meet with Salvadoran president to plead case of mistakenly deported man
item.description: Lawmakers are pushing to meet with Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele while he’s in Washington amid broader efforts to secure the return of a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in the Central American nation. Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) sent a letter to El Salvador’s ambassador to the U.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested in Georgia for threats against Tulsi Gabbard, her husband
item.description: Federal authorities arrested 25-year-old Aliakbar Mohammed Amin for allegedly sending threatening text messages to DNI Tulsi Gabbard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Marocco, mastermind behind dismantling of USAID projects, leaves State Department
item.description: Pete Marocco, who led the dismantling of USAID under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, departs as critics accuse him of chaos and foreign aid cuts that gutted U.S. global development programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bukele says Trump has 350 million Americans to 'liberate' by ending crime, terrorism
item.description: El Salvador's Nayib Bukele told President Donald Trump that he must "liberate" Americans from crime and that letting men in women's sports is "violence against women.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plans to celebrate America's 250th anniversary were underway. Then came the federal funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Community celebrations being planned to commemorate the nation's 250th anniversary next year are at risk of being significantly scaled back or canceled because of federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump's administration, according to multiple state humanities councils acr
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First on Fox: Republican launches second straight bid to flip Democrat-held Senate seat in key battleground
item.description: Former Rep. Mike Rogers, who was the GOP Senate nominee in Michigan, is launching a second straight Republican run for the Senate in the crucial Great Lakes battleground state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump takes swipe at Biden, says US will support Somalia against Houthis
item.description: President Trump warned Houthis to hide after removing red tape and empowering the U.S. military to protect the people of Somalia from terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military schools offer test case for Trump education reforms
item.description: Military academies could increasingly show what President Trump wants to see from public schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump in ‘excellent health,’ doctor says after annual exam
item.description: White House physician Sean Barbabella wrote in a memo. ”His active lifestyle continues to contribute significantly to his well-being.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans fear Trump’s trade war could lead to political wipeout
item.description: Republican lawmakers say there’s a good chance that President Trump's trade war will boomerang on Republicans politically in 2026, as rising prices and shrinking growth could offset other accomplishments by the GOP.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Fire at Pennsylvania Governor's Residence was 'act of arson': State Police
item.description: Police said Governor Josh Shapiro and his family were able to safely evacuate after a fire — believed to be an "act of arson" — broke out at the Harrisburg mansion.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin adds migrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The request from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem to place the migrants on the “Ineligible Master File” maintained by the SSA would bar migrants from receiving benefits or being able to work in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Florida education bill sparks debate over funds for advanced placement programs
item.description: Florida lawmakers battled it out on the House floor this week over a bill that could lead to significant funding cuts to education.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs could impact Tampa Bay shoppers, businesses by summer, economist says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Cheri Hudson owns clothing store Imagine That!!! in South Tampa. It has been her business for 25 years. She is a supporter of President Trump’s trade policies. “I trust Donald Trump. It's only going to be a matter of time. The tariffs are fair.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York bill aims to help homeless people and their pets stay together
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new state bill aims to expand access to co-sheltering, allowing homeless people and domestic abuse survivors to stay together with their pets during a housing crisis. Senate Bill S673, sponsored by New York state Sen. Monica R.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11

item.title: U.S. says it is now monitoring immigrants' social media for antisemitism
item.description: Effective immediately, the government says it will begin screening immigrant social media for activity that officials think indicates support for antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd, MPR News

item.title: ICE to open office on Rikers Island with Mayor Eric Adams' permission
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – ICE is allowed to open an office inside New York City’s biggest jail, according to an executive order signed by Deputy Mayor Randy Mastro. The order stated that the safety of the city has been jeopardized by gangs like MS-13 and Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Banning books prompts emotional testimony on both sides of proposed Nevada law
item.description: Parents, political groups and a member of the Clark County school board slammed legislation that is aimed at ending local decisions to pull books off school library shelves during a legislative hearing in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration plans to strip NYC of $188M for shelter program: Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Trump administration is planning to strip New York City of $188 million, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Tuesday. The $188 million includes more than $80 million that the Federal Emergency Management Agency clawed back from the city in February.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Proposed California bill seeks to extend last call until 4 a.m. in downtown areas
item.description: A new California bill would allow bars and restaurants in designated hospitality zones to serve alcohol until 4 a.m. on certain days, beginning Jan. 1, 2026.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
